title: World reacts to US, Israel attack on Iran, Tehran retaliation - Al Jazeera
summary: Global powers are closely monitoring the escalating conflict in the Middle East following a joint US and Israeli military action against Iran. Tehran's subsequent retaliation has heightened fears of a broader regional destabilization, prompting urgent calls for de-escalation from international bodies.

title: The World This Week | US Supreme Court strikes down Trump’s global tariffs, tensions with Iran reach a tipping point, India-Israel elevate ties - The Indian Express
summary: The US Supreme Court recently invalidated global tariffs imposed by the previous administration, signaling a significant shift in international trade policy. Concurrently, geopolitical tensions in the Middle East have intensified, while India and Israel have announced strengthened bilateral relations.

title: WHO issues global guideline on the use of GLP-1 medicines in treating obesity - World Health Organization (WHO)
summary: The World Health Organization has released new global guidelines concerning the application of GLP-1 medications for managing obesity. This significant policy aims to standardize treatment approaches and improve public health outcomes worldwide.

title: From Japan to Israel, elections are reshaping global politics - ABC News & Headlines – Australian Broadcasting Corporation
summary: A series of recent elections, spanning from Japan to Israel, are significantly altering the global political landscape. These electoral outcomes reflect evolving public sentiments and are expected to influence international relations and domestic policies across various nations.

title: Global Cooperation Is Changing, Not Declining: World Economic Forum - Vision IAS
summary: A recent analysis from the World Economic Forum suggests that global cooperation is undergoing a transformation rather than experiencing a decline. This perspective highlights a shift in the nature and mechanisms through which nations collaborate on international issues.

title: IT rules: India proposes new digital rules to regulate news and political posts on social media - BBC
summary: India is introducing new regulations aimed at governing content shared on social media platforms, specifically targeting news and political discourse. These proposed rules seek to establish a framework for oversight on digital posts, potentially impacting freedom of expression online.

title: MeitY secretary says every social media user posting on current affairs can come under I&B oversight - ThePrint
summary: A senior official from the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology has indicated that individuals posting about current events on social media could fall under the purview of the Information and Broadcasting Ministry. This statement suggests a broad interpretation of new digital rules, extending government oversight to a wide range of online content creators.

title: PM Modi Longest Serving Head of Government India - usthadian.com
summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has achieved a significant milestone, becoming the longest-serving head of government in India's history. This accomplishment reflects his enduring political tenure and leadership over an extended period.

title: How the Tenth Schedule regulates political defections - The Indian Express
summary: This article explains the Tenth Schedule of the Indian Constitution, which is designed to prevent political defections by elected representatives. It details the legal framework and implications for lawmakers who switch parties, aiming to ensure stability in legislative bodies.

title: Election Commission of India (ECI), Structure, Framework, Composition - Vajiram & Ravi
summary: This article provides an overview of the Election Commission of India, detailing its organizational structure, operational framework, and the composition of its members. title: Global Markets React to Rising Tensions and Economic Indicators - Devdiscourse
summary: Global financial markets are currently experiencing volatility as investors respond to escalating geopolitical tensions and a mixed bag of recent economic data. This dynamic environment is prompting caution among traders, who are closely monitoring global events for further direction.

title: Debt, AI boom and economic fragilities raise global risks, BIS says - CNBC
summary: The Bank for International Settlements (BIS) has issued a warning about increasing global economic vulnerabilities, citing the rapid expansion of artificial intelligence and mounting debt levels. These factors, alongside existing fragilities, are creating a complex landscape of heightened risks for the international financial system.
